articles.title: Bridgnorth Aluminium secures five-year battery foil supply deal with Lotte Aluminium Materials USA
articles.summary: Bridgnorth Aluminium (BAL), the UK's last remaining aluminium rolling mill, signed a five-year supply agreement with Lotte Aluminium Materials USA to deliver battery-grade aluminium foil for the EV battery sector; the contract was formalised in Elizabethtown, Kentucky and covers output from BAL's 368-person Shropshire facility.
articles.novelis_angle: A five-year dedicated EV battery foil offtake confirms that North American battery cell manufacturers are committing to long-term aluminium sourcing — a demand signal directly relevant to Novelis's Bay Minette plant commissioning in H2 2026.

articles.title: Aluminum's war shock blunted by dark transits and Chinese supply
articles.summary: The Iran war triggered one of aluminium's largest supply shocks, but the feared surge above $4,000/t did not materialise: Gulf smelters kept alumina flowing through the Strait of Hormuz via 'dark transit' vessels that disabled AIS tracking, with Persian Gulf alumina imports returning to pre-war levels by May 2026, while Chinese smelters operating at an annualised ~47 Mt — above the official 45 Mt cap — provided an additional supply buffer.
articles.novelis_angle: With the Hormuz peace framework signed 19 June, the risk premium embedded in European P1020 premiums should continue unwinding in H2 2026, reducing a key metal-cost headwind for Novelis's European plants.

articles.title: From bauxite diplomacy to aluminium's supply squeeze: how the global aluminium value chain is being reshaped
articles.summary: H1 2026 aluminium prices climbed 29.1% to a peak of $3,855/t on 2 June — the highest since March 2022 — driven by Middle East supply disruptions, with the analysis mapping structural upstream risks including India-Venezuela bauxite diplomacy, Guinea export uncertainty, and political scrutiny of 33 bauxite shipments from the Solomon Islands.
